title: Reference Secrets safely, never bake them in
description: Mount Secrets as files or use a secrets manager/CSI driver; avoid plaintext env, committed manifests, and over-broad Secret read access.
body: Use when a workload needs credentials or when reviewing Secret handling. Do not commit Secret manifests with real values, inject secrets as plaintext env that leak into logs/exec, or grant broad get/list on secrets. Actions: prefer mounting Secrets as files (tmpfs) or an external secrets manager via CSI; scope RBAC to the specific Secret; enable encryption-at-rest for etcd. Validation signals include no secret literals in version control, secrets absent from pod env dumps, and RBAC limited to named secrets. Risk boundary: any path that exposes a secret value in logs, env, or VCS is a blocking finding; escalate and remediate.
